schema.name: Taypro Solar Cleaning CAPEX vs OPEX Guide
schema.description: Procurement guide comparing robot CAPEX purchase with AMC versus Taypro managed OPEX cleaning for Indian utility solar plants.

hero.title: Solar cleaning CAPEX vs OPEX: which model fits your plant?
hero.subtitle: IPP and CPSU teams evaluate robot purchase (CAPEX + AMC) against OPEX (pay per panel cleaned). This page compares balance-sheet impact, mobilisation speed, audit trails, and 5-year TCO, not just sticker robot price.

stats.heading: CAPEX vs OPEX at a glance for Indian utility solar

card0.label: Per panel per cycle
card0.body: Volume-discounted on 10–100 MW+ fleets. See OPEX pricing page for indicative monthly bands.

card1.label: 10 MW wet-wash / year
card1.body: Industry benchmark for manual crew + water on arid utility sites, rises linearly with MW.

card2.label: Common IPP decision band
card2.body: Finance teams model both CAPEX depreciation and OPEX line items across multi-plant portfolios.

card3.label: Same Taypro fleet
card3.body: 50–500 MW references use robot purchase or managed OPEX: same GLYDE/NECTYR stack.

prose.heading: When to choose robot CAPEX vs OPEX
prose.p1: Robot CAPEX means you purchase GLYDE, GLYDE-X, NYUMA, NYUMA-X, or HELYX, depreciate the fleet on your balance sheet, and contract structured AMC for uptime. OPEX means Taypro retains fleet ownership, you pay monthly for panels actually cleaned, reconciled through NECTYR telemetry.
prose.p2: Many Indian IPPs start with OPEX to mobilise quickly without capital approval, then transition to owned fleets on long-hold assets after 3–5 years of proven PR recovery. CPSU and SECI-tendered plants often model both scenarios in parallel using the same soiling and tariff assumptions.
prose.p3: Compare on ₹ per recovered MWh over 5–20 years, not robot unit price alone. Manual wet washing on a 10 MW arid site typically runs ₹45–80 lakh per year all-in; robotic programmes convert cleaning to measurable per-panel economics with 3–10 dry cycles monthly.
prose.p4: Both commercial models include NECTYR block-level cleaning logs, the audit layer lenders and internal O&M governance ask for at 50 MW scale. Electrical O&M, inverters, and tracker mechanical work stay with your existing contractor; Taypro owns the cleaning execution layer.
prose.p5: Discount rates of 10–14% are common in Indian utility finance models when comparing 5-year TCO. Use the ROI calculator below with your MW, tariff, and installation type, then request a site-specific soiling study for binding terms.

capex.title: Robot CAPEX + AMC
capex.body: Purchase the fleet. Own depreciation, residual value, and spare inventory policy. Taypro commissions, trains operators, and maintains robots under structured AMC with NECTYR included.

opex.title: OPEX service
opex.body: No upfront robot purchase. Pay monthly for verified panels cleaned, ideal when balance-sheet efficiency and fast mobilisation matter more than asset ownership.

procurementComparison.heading: CAPEX vs OPEX procurement comparison
procurementComparison.intro: Use this matrix in RFQ and investment committee packs. Rates and CAPEX bands are directional, binding terms follow a site soiling study.

row0.capex: Capitalise fleet; depreciate over plant life
row0.opex: Operating expense; no robot asset on books

row1.capex: Commissioning + operator hire; capex approval cycle
row1.opex: Faster: Taypro owns and deploys fleet

row3.capex: You schedule; uptime and spare risk

row5.opex: 3–10 year IPP holds; balance-sheet sensitive sponsors

steps.heading: How to model CAPEX vs OPEX for your plant
steps.subheading: Five steps finance and O&M teams use before signing a cleaning contract.
steps.step0Body: MW capacity, state, fixed tilt vs tracker, module Wp, PPA or merchant tariff, hold period, and current annual cleaning spend.
steps.step1Body: Use regional benchmarks (8–25% seasonal loss without systematic cleaning) or a Taypro soiling study. Choose 3–10 cycles/month for dry robotic programmes.
steps.step2Title: Run parallel CAPEX and OPEX scenarios
steps.step2Body: Use the ROI calculator with both procurement models. Compare 5-year TCO at your discount rate (10–14% illustrative).
steps.step3Body: Confirm NECTYR reporting meets lender or SECI technical due diligence. Define breakdown response and coverage evidence.
steps.step4Body: Taypro engineers issue fleet sizing, indicative pricing, and mobilisation timeline, CAPEX bands or OPEX rates reconciled to site geometry.

highlight.heading: The finance question
highlight.lead: Are you optimising for balance sheet or for speed to PR recovery?
highlight.body: OPEX preserves gearing and mobilises in weeks. CAPEX suits long-hold owners who amortise robots over 15–20 years. Many portfolios use both, OPEX on new acquisitions, CAPEX on core assets.

eligibility.title: CAPEX vs OPEX: quick fit guide
eligibility.for0: You need fast mobilisation without capex committee approval
eligibility.for1: Hold period is under 10 years or balance-sheet gearing is constrained
eligibility.for2: You want opex tied to verified panels cleaned, not lump-sum crew contracts
eligibility.for3: Portfolio has mixed MW sizes and you want one accountable vendor
eligibility.not0: Hold period exceeds 15 years on 50 MW+ core assets
eligibility.not1: You have in-house O&M capacity to own spares and fleet depreciation
eligibility.not2: You already own robots and only need AMC + NECTYR upgrade

proseSecondary.heading: OPEX first, CAPEX later, a common IPP pattern
proseSecondary.p1: Several Indian IPPs contract OPEX for the first operating seasons to prove PR recovery and NECTYR audit trails, then transition to owned fleets on assets they plan to hold 20+ years.
proseSecondary.p2: SECI and state discom tenders increasingly ask for evidence of cleaning coverage, not just a line item for manual washing. Both CAPEX and OPEX models deliver NECTYR block-level logs; the difference is who owns the robot asset.
proseSecondary.p3: Tracker plants carry higher per-panel economics (1.8× OPEX multiplier vs fixed tilt). Model this in both scenarios before committee sign-off.

faq.heading: Solar cleaning CAPEX vs OPEX: FAQ
faq.subheading: Common questions from IPP finance and O&M procurement teams.
faq.q0: What is the difference between CAPEX and OPEX for solar cleaning?
faq.a0: CAPEX means purchasing robots and owning depreciation plus AMC. OPEX means Taypro owns the fleet and you pay monthly for panels cleaned, verified in NECTYR.
faq.q1: When does OPEX beat CAPEX for a 50 MW plant?
faq.a1: When hold period is shorter, capex approval is slow, or you want opex aligned to verified cleaning volume. Run both scenarios in the ROI calculator with your tariff.
faq.q2: Can we switch from OPEX to CAPEX mid-contract?
faq.a2: Hybrid transitions are common after 3–5 years of proven performance. Discuss fleet purchase options during commercial engagement.
faq.q3: Does OPEX include operators and spares?
faq.a3: Yes. OPEX is all-in for operators, robots, spares, SOPs, and NECTYR: you pay for panels cleaned, not idle hours.
faq.q4: How do lenders view robotic cleaning OPEX?
faq.a4: Many prefer auditable per-panel billing with NECTYR coverage logs over opaque manual crew contracts.
faq.q5: What discount rate should we use for TCO?
faq.a5: Indian utility models often use 10–14% illustrative. Your treasury team should set the rate for investment committee packs.
faq.q6: Is manual cleaning cheaper than robots?
faq.a6: Manual wet costs on 10 MW arid sites often run ₹45–80 lakh/year and scale linearly with MW. Compare on ₹ per recovered MWh over 5 years.
faq.q7: Do tracker plants change the CAPEX vs OPEX decision?
faq.a7: Tracker geometry adds cost per cycle (1.8× OPEX multiplier). Both models remain viable, model tracker-specific inputs in the calculator.
faq.q8: What is the minimum plant size for OPEX?
faq.a8: OPEX programmes typically start around 10 MW utility scale. Smaller sites may face minimum monthly programme fees.
faq.a9: Yes. Robot CAPEX programmes include NECTYR fleet portal for scheduling, coverage audits, and AMC ticketing.
faq.q10: How do SECI tenders treat cleaning commercial models?
faq.a10: Tenders increasingly require documented cleaning coverage. Both models can meet technical diligence with NECTYR evidence, choose based on balance-sheet preference.
faq.q11: Where do I get binding pricing?
faq.a11: This page is a decision guide. Request a site quote after a soiling study, OPEX rates or CAPEX bands are issued per plant geometry.
